mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or median 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio is below -0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which forces equity repricing and generates negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and rising corporate revenues, which directly supports positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO month-over-month changes yield negative median 5-day forward returns, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 70% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the signal shows no statistical edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates deteriorating labor market quality, which reduces aggregate demand and drives negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to achieve a positive out-of-sample R-squared, or 60-day forward returns are positive in the majority of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger economic activity and corporate profit expansion, which mechanically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production greater than 1.0 percent signals severe supply-side contraction, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when INDPRO falls below -1.0%, or the threshold signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.
